clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
abdominal pain 
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
___ year old ___ speaking man with history of a 
non-ischemic dilated cardiomyopathy s/p DT Heartmate II LVAD 
implant on ___ for ___ class IV heart failure complicated 
by RV dysfunction and ventricular arrhythmias. He was previously 
declined for heart
transplantation at ___ and ___ due to morbid obesity and BMI
however ___ has been able to lose weight and keep it off since
LVAD implant and ___ has agreed to list him for heart
transplantation. 

He also has a known umbilical hernia for which he has weekly or 
bi-weekly symptoms.  The hernia usually reduces.  Yesterday 
(___) at 1 pm, he had his typical hernia pain but it remained 
constant which is why he presented to the ED.  He endorsed 
vomiting twice at home and once in the Emergency department.  

In the ED, his vitals were HR: 80  BP:70/Doppler (LVAD) RR: 20 
Sp02: 98% RA.  He denied any fevers, chest pain, shortness of 
breath. He had a CT A/P w/o contrast was concerning for early 
small bowel obstruction.  Surgery consulted, however, in the 
interim--his hernia was able to be reduced and he also had a 
large bowel movement with resolution of his symptoms. He was 
able to eat a meal prior to coming to the floor at 1530. 


[... truncated ...]

Social History:
___
Family History:
Mother with heart disease
Father who is healthy

Hospital Course:
___ year old man with a history of non-ischemic dilated 
cardiomyopathy s/p DT Heartmate II LVAD implant on ___ for 
___ class IV heart failure complicated by RV dysfunction and 
ventricular arrhythmias. He presented to the ED on the day of 
admission with a periumbilical hernia which was causing pain, 
nausea, and vomiting. It was reduced in the ED and he was 
admitted for observation and possible elective hernia repair but 
it was determined that this would happened as an outpatient.
 
# Periumbilical Hernia: Episode of possible incarceration with 
CT showing signs of early SBO, resolved with reduction in ED. 
Patient was planned for elective repair as an outpatient. 

# Dilated, non-ischemic cardiomyopathy with reduced EF: Patient 
s/p HeartMate II LVAD. Patient maintained on warfarin with goal 
INR ___ on outpatient regimen.  

# Refractory VT: Mr. ___ had 17 beats of VT and was 
overdrive paced out of the rhythm and was asymptomatic.  EP was 
consulted regarding need for change in medical management and it 
was determined that he was already on maximal medical therapy 
with quinidine, mexilitine, and metoprolol. 

# ___: Mr. ___ developed ___ with creatinine of 1.5 on ___ 
from baseline of 1.2. Creatinine improved with improved PO 
intake. 

# Pulmonary HTN secondary to dilated cardiomyopathy: Patient 
continued on sildenafil 20mg PO TID.

# Diabetes Mellitus: Blood sugars well controlled on ISS.

# Gout: Patient continued on colchicine and febuxostat.

Transitional Issu

Chief Complaint:
elective periumbilical hernia repair surgery
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
___ Umbilical hernia repair with mesh

History of Present Illness:
___ year old ___ speaking man with history of a 
non-ischemic dilated cardiomyopathy s/p DT Heartmate II LVAD 
implant on ___ for ___ class IV heart failure complicated 
by RV dysfunction and ventricular arrhythmias, and T2DM, CVA in 
___ w/ no residual deficits. He was recently admitted for 
abdominal pain ___ - ___, thought to be due to 
incarceration of his umbilical hernia. He presents for planned 
admission for umbilical hernia repair in setting of LVAD. 

Re his abdominal pain and hernia, he has a known umbilical 
hernia for which he has weekly or bi-weekly symptoms.  The 
hernia usually reduces.  On ___, his hernia pain was unusually 
persistent and he developed vomiting; he presented to ___ ED 
and was admitted ___. CT in the ED showed signs of early 
SBO; pain and other sxs resolved with reduction in ED, and he 
was planned for elective repair as an outpatient. Given LVAD, he 
is admitted for heparin bridging. Patient states that since 
discharge he has not had any abdominal pain. Discharge weight 
last hospitalization 121.9kg.

Social History:
___
Family History:
Mother with heart disease
Father who is healthy

Hospital Course:
ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
___ year old ___ speaking man with history of a 
non-ischemic dilated cardiomyopathy s/p DT Heartmate II LVAD 
implant on ___ for ___ class IV heart failure complicated 
by RV dysfunction and ventricular arrhythmias, and T2DM, CVA in 
___ w/ no residual deficits. Presents for planned admission for 
hernia repair with heparin bridging in stg of LVAD. 

# Umbilical hernia repair: Presented for planned admission for 
elective umbilical hernia repair with heparin bridging in the 
setting of LVAD. Patient's home warfarin was held and a heparin 
drip was started when INR was below 2.0. He underwent successful 
hernia repair and post procedure restarted on warfarin without 
any complications on a modified dose. Pain was controlled with 
oral oxycodone, Tylenol, and a bowel regimen with senna and 
docusate was started. Patient was discharged on warfarin. The 
rest of his medications were not modified.
Post surgery patient developed a hematoma underneath his 
umbilical hernia site confirmed by a CT scan with decreased 
hemoglobin which stabilized. Initially received vancomycin but 
was stopped as there was no evidence of infection with negative 
chest x-ray, blood, and urine cultures. Surgery felt that it was 
thrombosed hematoma and there was no concern for infection or 
expansion and that patient will follow up as an outpatient in 
surgery clinic.

# Severe sCHF (L and R-sided) s/p DT Heartmate II LVAD: Has been 
stable. No evidence of hypervolemia c

Hospital Course:
___ man with recent hernia repair and non-ischemic 
dilated cardiomyopathy s/p DT Heartmate II LVAD implant on 
___ for ___ class IV heart failure complicated by RV 
dysfunction and ventricular arrhythmias, presenting to the ED 
with oozing from his hernia repair site.

#Status post hernia repair: He was recently admitted for heparin 
bridging during umbilical hernia repair on ___ hospital 
course notable for a surgical site hematoma without hemodynamic 
compromise. He was discharged home and returned with 
strikethrough on his dressing and was admitted for bleeding 
evaluation. Per Surgical consult, "No hematoma or signs of wound 
infection. Manual pressure held for 10 min with resolution of 
bleeding, a stitch and steri strips were placed by the surgical 
team and pressure dressing applied." His hemoglobin was stable. 
He underwent CT abomen and pelvis to ensure no complications, 
which showed stable hematoma size, and was discharged home. He 
has follow up with his surgeon, Dr. ___, in one week. 

#Hyperkalemia: Patient's home potassium supplementation was 
discontinued given that he is no longer on a potassium wasting 
diuretic and had minor potassium elevation that resolved without 
any intervention in the ED.

TRANSITIONAL ISSUES
===================
[] recheck renal panel in ___ weeks to evaluate electrolyte 
levels
[] recheck CBC to make sure it is stable to improving
[] check abdominal surgical wound for healing

Discharge Weight: 124.7kg

 
Medications on Admission:

___ year old male with chief complaint of ICD shock this am. He

transplantation. He felt the shock around 7:40am on ___ after 
taking his AM medications. Reports he is at his baseline dyspnea 
on exertion, able to walk 3 minutes or up 2 flights of stairs, 
though this causes to feel "very tired." Reports twice daily 
lightheadedness with position changes though there has been no 
recent changes in this phenomenon. Endorses chronic R>L knee 
pain.

ED COURSE
In the ED intial vitals were: 98.1 80 80 16 97% RA  
EKG: V paced at 80 
Labs/studies notable for: K 3.6, Mg 1.8, mildly elevated alk 
phos at 189, Cr 1.2 (baseline), Hg 12.4 (above recent baseline), 
INR therapeutic at 2.4
Patient was given: 40meq PO K, 2mg IV Mg  
Vitals on transfer:  98.0 80/D 81 16 95%RA

On the floor...no complaints, denies chest pain, dizziness, 
lightheadedness.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: On review of systems, denies any prior 
history of  deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, bleeding

Hospital Course:
___ h/o non-ischemic dilated cardiomyopathy s/p DT Heartmate II 
LVAD implant on ___ for ___ class IV heart failure 
complicated by RV dysfunction and ventricular arrhythmias 
presenting after ICD shock on ___ AM. EP interrogation in ED 
found multiple episodes of VT with TCLs ~350-420ms from 
___ all successfully treated with ATP except for 1 
episode today at 6:42AM requiring ICD shock.

1. NIDCM ___ class IV s/p LVAD p/w ICD shock - listed at ___ 
for heart transplant
- EP evaluated in ED, no EP interventions at this time and 
recommended consideration of amiodarone if recurrent shocks, 
however this has not been successful in the past. No clear 
precipitating factor. Electrolytes were maintained K>4.5, Mg 
>2.5. Continued home mexilitine, quinidine, metoprolol, 
sildenafil, dipyrimadole, aspirin 325, Coumadin (4mg TuThSatSun, 
3mg ___, daily INR, goal ___, ferrous sulfate 325, PO 
magnesium. Underwent uncomplicated L stellate ganglion block to 
help prevent/delay recurrence as this has been successful in the 
past. INR 3.1 on discharge, pt instructed to take 3mg Coumadin 
that evening and the next, then resume his usual schedule. 
Outpatient labs and follow-up arranged for 2 days post-discharge 
with Cardiology.

2. Chronic pain - osteoarthritis/knee pain
Continued on oxycodone 10mg q12H for knee pain and tramadol with 
doses/frequency consistent with pre-existing outpatient 
narcotics contract. Not discharged with any additional pain 
medication
